engine.ladder_reasoning: No defined expertise sold (not trust), no portfolio-as-proof (not work), and it is a packaged-goods manufacturer brand rather than a venue you visit or a menu you browse to decide (not place). Visitor is evaluating the product lines, so thing.

goal.one_action: Contact the company via the customer-service/commercial/careers form (or phone), after browsing product lines and recipe videos

structure.archetype_note: Classic brand + product-catalog site for a food manufacturer: hero banner, recipe video showcase, product-line grid (no prices/cart), company history, contact form. Brochureware, not e-commerce.

mapped.observed_as: RECEITAS PERFEITAS PARA TODAS AS OCASIÕES
SELECIONE UM DE NOSSOS PRODUTOS! (7 product-line cards)

proposed_new.name: content-index
proposed_new.evidence: Home shows a grid of 6 embedded YouTube recipe videos and the product/recipes area functions as a browsable recipe feed — a content index, not blog posts.

confidence.why: fetch_quality is full; all 4 pages, nav, forms, CTAs, and proof/commerce flags captured clearly. Brand-catalog archetype is unambiguous.

gap_findings: Catalog with product-line subcategory drill-down but NO prices and NO cart — brochure catalog, distinct from storefront-checkout; closed vocab 'catalog' covers browse-only, adequate.

Contact form doubles as a job-application form (Departamento dropdown incl. 'RH - Vagas' + resume upload) — a single form serving multiple intents (SAC/commercial/marketing/careers); current lead-form capability does not express department-routing or careers intent.
